comment_text.sn24.3:1.3: This is eternalism (sassatavāda), see SN 22.81:10.7, SN 12.17:5.1.

root_text.sn24.3:1.2: “Kismiṁ nu kho, bhikkhave, sati, kiṁ upādāya, kiṁ abhinivissa evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjati:
root_text.sn24.3:1.3: ‘so attā, so loko, so pecca bhavissāmi nicco dhuvo sassato avipariṇāmadhammo’”ti?
root_text.sn24.3:2.1: “Rūpe kho, bhikkhave, sati, rūpaṁ upādāya, rūpaṁ abhinivissa evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjati:
root_text.sn24.3:2.2: ‘so attā, so loko, so pecca bhavissāmi nicco dhuvo sassato avipariṇāmadhammo’ti.
root_text.sn24.3:2.6: viññāṇe sati, viññāṇaṁ upādāya, viññāṇaṁ abhinivissa evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjati:
root_text.sn24.3:2.7: ‘so attā, so loko, so pecca bhavissāmi nicco dhuvo sassato avipariṇāmadhammo’ti.
root_text.sn24.3:3.4: api nu taṁ anupādāya evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjeyya:
root_text.sn24.3:3.13: api nu taṁ anupādāya evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjeyya:
root_text.sn24.3:3.17: “Yampidaṁ diṭṭhaṁ sutaṁ mutaṁ viññātaṁ pattaṁ pariyesitaṁ anuvicaritaṁ manasā tampi niccaṁ vā aniccaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn24.3:3.19: api nu taṁ anupādāya evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjeyya:
root_text.sn24.3:3.20: ‘so attā, so loko, so pecca bhavissāmi nicco dhuvo sassato avipariṇāmadhammo’”ti?
root_text.sn24.3:4.1: “Yato kho, bhikkhave, ariyasāvakassa imesu ca ṭhānesu kaṅkhā pahīnā hoti, dukkhepissa kaṅkhā pahīnā hoti …pe… dukkhanirodhagāminiyā paṭipadāyapissa kaṅkhā pahīnā hoti—
root_text.sn24.3:4.2: ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako sotāpanno avinipātadhammo niyato sambodhiparāyano”ti.

translation_text.sn24.3:1.2: “Mendicants, when what exists, because of grasping what and insisting on what, does the view arise:
translation_text.sn24.3:1.3: ‘The self and the cosmos are one and the same. After death I will be that, permanent, everlasting, eternal, and imperishable’?”
translation_text.sn24.3:1.4: “Our teachings are rooted in the Buddha. …”
translation_text.sn24.3:2.1: “When form exists, because of grasping form and insisting on form, the view arises:
translation_text.sn24.3:2.2: ‘The self and the cosmos are one and the same. After death I will be that, permanent, everlasting, eternal, and imperishable.’
translation_text.sn24.3:2.6: consciousness exists, because of grasping consciousness and insisting on consciousness, the view arises:
translation_text.sn24.3:2.7: ‘The self and the cosmos are one and the same. After death I will be that, permanent, everlasting, eternal, and imperishable.’
translation_text.sn24.3:3.17: “That which is seen, heard, thought, known, attained, sought, and explored by the mind: is that permanent or impermanent?”
translation_text.sn24.3:3.19: “But by not grasping what’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, would such a view arise?”
translation_text.sn24.3:4.1: “When a noble disciple has given up doubt regarding these six grounds, and has given up doubt in suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the practice that leads to its cessation,
translation_text.sn24.3:4.2: they’re called a noble disciple who is a stream-enterer, not liable to be reborn in the underworld, destined for awakening.”
